assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 27: '-1 test function removed: `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` was completely removed.' Coverage for qualifier-specific recommendation behavior is lost.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 28: 'In `test_recommend_purls_basic`, the assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"`) to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`). This is a weaker assertion because it matches a shorter, less specific string.' Reductive Findings line 54 also reiterates this: 'the assertion was relaxed from checking a fully qualified PURL string ... to a versioned PURL without qualifiers ... This is a specificity reduction in the assertion.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)

No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) are present in the outputs. assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and advisory notes about MIXED test classification, but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command output, and no indication that a merge was performed. The report presents findings (verification table, test change classification details, acceptance criteria verification) for human review. The final line states: 'New tests adequately cover the simplified behavior.' — a finding, not an action.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 21: 'This classification is produced by Style/Conventions domain analysis, based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content for modified test files, and identifying new test files.' assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 21 states: 'based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content for modified test files'. The Structural Summary (lines 26-41) describes concrete file-level changes: removed function 'test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers' which 'existed on the base branch (lines 30-48 of the base-branch file)', assertion changes comparing specific PURL strings, added assertions with exact code, new test functions. The Semantic Assessment (lines 44-49) discusses 'test coverage for qualifier-specific behavior' and structural facts about assertion weakening — all referencing test file content, not acceptance criteria. The acceptance criteria are in a separate section ('Acceptance Criteria Verification') and are not referenced in the classification analysis.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination

All test functions have doc comments. Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews).' The report states Eval Quality is N/A with the reason 'no eval result reviews', but it does NOT mention the specific 3-criteria detection mechanism (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).
